methodology.score: mean across (shape × scale × format) of (tokens(format)/tokens(eslint-stylish) − 1), as percent

effectiveness.slo: structured formats (interlace-json, eslint-json, eslint-json-with-metadata) score 4.0/4 on every fixture

latency.slo: interlace-* P50 ≤ {tiny:5, small:10, medium:25, large:50, extreme:250} ms

methodology.sloHeadline: interlace-compact ≤ eslint-stylish on token cost; structured formats signalScore = 4.0 on every fixture; interlace-* P50 latency under per-scale ceiling.

model.name: o200k_base + cl100k_base (gpt-tokenizer)
model.role: No LLM is called by this bench. The tokenizer stands in for the consumer model on the cost axis. For LLM-call benches, see ilb-llm-fix and ilb-ai.

items.name: @interlace/eslint-formatter
items.role: subject under test — three modes (human, compact, json) loaded from compiled dist (5 mode(s) active this run)

items.name: eslint
items.role: industry baseline + comparators — built-in formatters loaded directly via require: eslint-stylish, eslint-json, eslint-json-with-metadata, eslint-html

items.name: gpt-tokenizer
items.role: cost-axis tokenizer — o200k_base (headline, GPT-4o/Claude proxy) + cl100k_base (sanity, GPT-3.5/4 proxy)

items.name: node:process.hrtime.bigint
items.role: latency-axis measurement — median-of-5 samples per cell after one warm-up render

items.name: tsx
items.role: TypeScript runtime — runs the suite without a separate transpile step
